office_does: One of two state representatives for the 30th Legislative District (Federal Way and nearby south King County communities), serving a two-year term in the Washington State House.
race_blurb: Incumbent Democrat Jamila E. Taylor, chair of the House Civil Rights & Judiciary Committee, faces Republican first-time candidate Tiffany Bowyer, a small business owner running on affordability and spending accountability. Both advance to the November general election.

candidates.summary: Incumbent Democratic state representative seeking a third term, chair of the House Civil Rights & Judiciary Committee and an attorney with a legal-aid and victim-advocacy background.
highlights: Cites passing laws on gun-violence reduction, police training and trust, small-business tax reduction, and working-family tax credit expansion [S1]
Campaign highlights homeownership-access legislation and remediation of racially restrictive property deeds [S2]
Endorsed by the 30th District Democrats, State Labor Council, Planned Parenthood, OneAmerica Votes, and Sierra Club; campaign site cites a Seattle Times endorsement [S1][S2]

experience.basis: Third-term incumbent and committee chair with a cited record of passed legislation; opponent is a first-time candidate.

safety.basis: Record emphasizes root causes of crime, gun-violence reduction, and police training/trust reform rather than enforcement-first measures.

taxes.basis: Record is targeted relief in both directions — small-business tax reductions and expanded working-family tax credits — with no stated position on new progressive revenue.

housing.basis: Emphasizes public resources for housing, a new homeownership program, and remediation of discriminatory deed covenants.

spending.basis: Platform leads with delivering resources for housing, childcare, and expanded tax credits — an investment orientation.

social.basis: Chairs the Civil Rights & Judiciary Committee and vice-chairs the Members of Color Caucus; endorsed by Planned Parenthood and OneAmerica Votes.

candidates.summary: Republican first-time candidate from Federal Way, a small business owner with a health-and-wellness background running on affordability, spending accountability, and police staffing.
highlights: Opposes new taxes on working families and businesses; says government spending growth is driving up property taxes, gas prices, and utility bills [S1][S2]
Calls for transparent tracking of taxpayer funds and program performance audits, citing untracked childcare funding and grant-oversight gaps [S2]
Proposes fully staffing police departments and holding repeat offenders accountable, coordinated with behavioral-health services [S2]

taxes.basis: Repeated explicit opposition to new taxes on working families and businesses across pamphlet and campaign site.

spending.basis: Names spending growth as the driver of costs and calls specifically for transparent fund tracking and program performance audits.

safety.basis: Leads with fully staffing police and repeat-offender accountability, tempered by proposed coordination with behavioral-health services.

experience.basis: First-time candidate with no elected experience or legislative record, running against an incumbent committee chair.
